maulgar.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (1,274) ran below the Top 100 average (1,970), and overheal (59.4%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (53.4%) by 6 points - despite that, this was a strong 86th-percentile kill, ranking 1st of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank and 1st of 4 by raw healing (Maulgar's raw-healing comparison population is one smaller than the ilvl one - a real, single-fight endpoint difference, not an error) on this very short (37-second) burst-damage encounter.
maulgar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share ran 45.7 points ahead of the Top 100 average - the second-largest composition gap of the raid night.
maulgar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: No tracked cooldown was cast at all this kill (Innervate, Nature's Swiftness, and Swiftmend all show real zero counts) - a real, if unsurprising, gap on a fight this short, where there may simply not have been time to use any of them. The one real death (Pointsetta) has no cooldown to compare against as a result.
maulgar.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (100% vs. 97.6% average) tracked close to the Top 100 norm, but top-target concentration (27.6% vs. 46.3% average, -18.7) ran well below typical - healing was spread more evenly across the 5 real targets touched than the benchmark usually shows on this fight.
maulgar.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed out of real danger all kill (only 0.0% of the fight under 20%), ending around 65.9%. Spent 30.5% of the kill sitting above 90% mana, room to have healed more aggressively.

gruul.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (650) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,300, a 50% ratio), and overheal (56.7%) sits close to the sample's single worst parse (58.5%, a 1.8-point gap) - a real, below-average kill at the 58th percentile, with 1 real death logged.
gruul.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share ran a modest 9.2 points ahead of the Top 100 average - one of the smaller composition gaps of the raid night.
gruul.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Swiftmend (86%-used norm) and Mana Potion (55%-used norm) both went uncast this kill - real gaps. Rebirth was cast on Vinnyvoss about a minute after Vinnyvoss's own death - a real, if delayed, response.
gruul.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (83.2% vs. 68.1% average, +15.1) and top-target concentration (54% vs. 36.1% average, +17.9) both ran well above the Top 100 norm, across 11 distinct targets.
gruul.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed comfortable for most of the kill (only 3.6% of the fight under 20%) but was driven down to 5.5% by the end - a late squeeze, not a sustained one.

hydross.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (510) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,269, a 40% ratio - the weakest of the entire raid night), and overheal (70.5%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (58%) by 12.5 points - the largest overheal gap of the raid night - landing at the 25th percentile, one of the raid's three lowest kills, with 2 real deaths logged.
hydross.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share ran 10.9 points ahead of the Top 100 average - a real but middling gap.
hydross.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Rebirth was cast on Staubz just 10.4 seconds after Staubz's own death - a real, fast save, though a real overcast against a norm where only 10% of parses use it here. Deepbowlz's death shortly after wasn't answered by any further cooldown - the only Rebirth available had already been used.
hydross.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (92.5% vs. 83.3% average) tracked close to the Top 100 norm, while top-target concentration (37.5% vs. 47.9% average, -10.4) ran below typical, across 9 distinct targets.
hydross.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed comfortable for most of the kill (only 11.9% of the fight under 20%) but was driven down to 10.8% by the end - a late squeeze, not a sustained one.

leotheras.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (834) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,517), and overheal (61.9%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (54.3%) by 7.6 points - with 9 real deaths logged, the most of any kill this raid night by a wide margin, though this still landed at a respectable 67th percentile.
leotheras.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Rejuvenation's share ran 9.2 points ahead of the Top 100 average - a real, modest gap, and one of only two kills this raid night where Rejuvenation (not Lifebloom) is the largest composition gap.
leotheras.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Only one Swiftmend was cast the entire kill, and it's the 'nearest' cooldown to every one of the 9 real deaths purely because it's the only one logged (12.8 to 50.4 seconds removed) - not a real save for any of them. No Rebirth was attempted despite 9 deaths on a kill that ultimately succeeded, meaning real time remained afterward - a real, plausible missed opportunity.
leotheras.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (60.5% vs. 51% average, +9.5) ran modestly above the Top 100 norm, with 22 distinct targets touched - the widest spread of the raid night, consistent with a chaotic, high-death fight.
leotheras.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Spent 29.0% of this kill below 20% mana (26 casts made that low), a real efficiency squeeze rather than a one-off dip.

karathress.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (1,039) ran below the Top 100 average (1,834), and overheal (53.9%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (46.5%) by 7.4 points - with 2 real deaths logged, still a middling 53rd-percentile kill.
karathress.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share ran 16.2 points ahead of the Top 100 average - a real, moderate gap.
karathress.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Rebirth was cast on Staubz about a minute after Staubz's own death - a real, if slow, response, and a real overcast against a norm where only 11% of parses use it here. Zuggnuggs's death landed almost exactly as a Swiftmend (aimed at a different target) was cast - coincidental timing, not a save.
karathress.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (69.3% vs. 74% average) and top-target concentration (25.8% vs. 34.5% average) both tracked modestly below the Top 100 norm, across 18 distinct targets.
karathress.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Spent 20.5% of this kill below 20% mana (16 casts made that low), a real efficiency squeeze rather than a one-off dip.

morogrim.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (935) ran below the Top 100 average (1,442), and overheal (53.9%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (51.1%) by a real but narrow 2.8 points - a solid 71st-percentile kill, with 3 real deaths logged.
morogrim.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Rejuvenation's share ran 7.1 points ahead of the Top 100 average - the smallest composition gap of the raid night, and (like Leotheras) a case where Rejuvenation rather than Lifebloom leads.
morogrim.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched the Top 100 sample - no real deviation. None of the 3 real deaths (Shlicktree, Deepbowlz, Vinnyvoss, the latter two dying within a millisecond of each other) land close enough to a Swiftmend cast aimed at them specifically to call a real save; no Rebirth was attempted, though the norm here is a real, low 18%.
morogrim.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (68.2% vs. 67.4% average) and top-target concentration (42.6% vs. 38.5% average) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across 20 distinct targets - one of the raid's more balanced profiles.
morogrim.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed out of real danger all kill (only 6.7% of the fight under 20%), ending around 22.0%. A second Mana Potion use would have been available before the kill ended based on the first use's timing, but wasn't taken.

lurker.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (710) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,605, a 44% ratio), and overheal (51.5%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (49.1%) by a real but narrow 2.4 points - landing at the 39th percentile, one of the raid's three lowest kills, though with zero real deaths logged.
lurker.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth's share ran 19 points ahead of the Top 100 average - the second-largest non-Lifebloom composition gap of the raid night, and the only kill where Regrowth (rather than Lifebloom or Rejuvenation) leads.
lurker.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched the Top 100 sample in count. Swiftmend was self-cast rather than the usual other-target pattern (only 8% of the Top 100 sample casts it on themselves here) - a real, if minor, target-choice difference.
lurker.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (73.6% vs. 56.8% average, +16.8) ran well above the Top 100 norm, across 16 distinct targets, on a clean kill with no real deaths. This is also the one kill this raid night with a real, fight-specific gear swap (fishing pole and lure) - see the raid overview.
lurker.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed comfortable for most of the kill (only 6.2% of the fight under 20%) but was driven down to 0.0% by the end - a late squeeze, not a sustained one. A second Mana Potion use would have been available before the kill ended based on the first use's timing, but wasn't taken.

vashj.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (610) ran below the Top 100 average (1,139), and overheal (54.9%) sits right at the sample's single worst parse (53.5%, a narrow 1.4-point gap) - on the raid's longest kill this night (543 seconds) and its second-heaviest death count (6, including Danceswtrees's own), landing at the 41st percentile.
vashj.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share ran 22.5 points ahead of the Top 100 average - a real, sizeable gap, consistent with this raid night's broader pattern.
vashj.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Rebirth was cast on Captinspanky just 4.5 seconds after Captinspanky's own death - the fastest, cleanest save of the raid night. Danceswtrees's own death followed less than 6 seconds after that same Rebirth cast, with no further cooldown available to answer it. The remaining 3 deaths (Purehentai, Staubz, Deepbowlz, all within a second of each other) cluster near the end of the fight, far from any real cooldown - coincidental timing, not a missed save.
vashj.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (60.6% vs. 50.7% average, +9.9) and top-target concentration (31.6% vs. 21% average, +10.6) both ran above the Top 100 norm, across 21 distinct targets - the second-widest spread of the raid night.
vashj.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Spent 25.0% of this kill below 20% mana (52 casts made that low), a real efficiency squeeze rather than a one-off dip.

alar.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (736) ran below the Top 100 average (1,077), and overheal (59.8%) sits close to the sample's single worst parse (61.1%, a 1.3-point gap) - yet this was a strong 85th-percentile kill, with zero real deaths logged.
alar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share ran 15.3 points ahead of the Top 100 average - a real, moderate gap.
alar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched or exceeded the Top 100 sample - 4 real Swiftmends (including one self-cast), the heaviest Swiftmend count of the raid night alongside Kael'thas and Vashj. No real deaths to answer.
alar.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (92.9% vs. 78.9% average, +14.0) and top-target concentration (54.3% vs. 43.7% average, +10.6) both ran above the Top 100 norm, across 9 distinct targets, on a clean kill.
alar.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed comfortable for most of the kill (only 3.4% of the fight under 20%) but was driven down to 6.6% by the end - a late squeeze, not a sustained one.

voidreaver.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (1,423) ran below the Top 100 average (2,458), and overheal (36.8%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (33%) by 3.8 points - with 2 real deaths logged, a middling 61st-percentile kill.
voidreaver.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share ran 18.3 points ahead of the Top 100 average - a real, moderate gap.
voidreaver.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Swiftmend went uncast against an 80%-used Top 100 norm - the one real cooldown gap this kill. Neither of the 2 real deaths (Captinspanky, Staubz) shows a genuine save - the closest timings (both Innervate, a self-buff cooldown) actually landed after each death, not before.
voidreaver.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (52% vs. 37.2% average, +14.8) ran above the Top 100 norm, with top-target concentration (13.8% vs. 9.4% average) tracking close to typical, across 17 distinct targets.
voidreaver.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Spent 31.8% of this kill below 20% mana (26 casts made that low), a real efficiency squeeze rather than a one-off dip.

solarian.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (664) ran below the Top 100 average (1,225), yet this landed at a strong 85th percentile - overheal (56.3%) actually ran 9.1 points better than the sample's single worst parse (65.4%), the only kill this raid night where overheal comfortably cleared that bar. Only 1 real death logged.
solarian.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth's share ran 37.7 points ahead of the Top 100 average - the largest composition gap of the entire raid night, and (with Lurker) one of two kills where Regrowth rather than Lifebloom leads.
solarian.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Swiftmend went uncast against a 90%-used Top 100 norm - a real, notable gap given how close to universal its use is in the benchmark. Rebirth was cast on Captinspanky about 17.6 seconds after Captinspanky's own death - a real, fast save, though a real overcast against a norm where only 9% of parses use it here.
solarian.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (52.3% vs. 52.8% average) and top-target concentration (16.4% vs. 16.4% average) both landed almost exactly on the Top 100 norm, across 15 distinct targets - a textbook-average target-distribution profile.
solarian.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed out of real danger all kill (only 0.0% of the fight under 20%), ending around 27.5%.

kaelthas.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (512) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,014), and overheal (72.8%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (61.7%) by 11.1 points - the highest raw overheal percentage of the raid night - landing at the 30th percentile, one of the raid's three lowest kills, with 6 real deaths logged on the raid's second-longest fight (487 seconds).
kaelthas.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share ran 28.7 points ahead of the Top 100 average - one of the larger composition gaps of the raid night.
kaelthas.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Rebirth went completely uncast against a real 62%-used Top 100 norm here - one of the highest baseline Rebirth-usage rates of any boss this raid night - despite 6 real deaths on a kill that ultimately succeeded, meaning real time remained afterward. A real, notable gap. None of the 6 deaths show a genuine save from any other cooldown either - the closest timings (Nature's Swiftness and Swiftmend) mostly landed before the deaths they're nearest to, not after, and the one cooldown that did land after a death (Innervate, 74 milliseconds after Staubz's) is a self-buff, not a save tool.
kaelthas.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (74.7% vs. 58.4% average, +16.3) ran well above the Top 100 norm, with 23 distinct targets touched - the widest spread of the entire raid night, consistent with a long, high-death fight.
kaelthas.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed out of real danger all kill (only 1.7% of the fight under 20%), ending around 35.9%. Spent 26.3% of the kill sitting above 90% mana, room to have healed more aggressively.

RaidOverview.GEAR_CONSISTENCY_FINDING: consistent, aside from one likely-benign single-kill gear swap (MainHand/OffHand on Lurker)
RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: The Back slot (item 28765) carries no permanent enchant across all 12 kills this raid night - the one persistent, real gear gap. The Feet slot enchant (fixed mid-raid on Danceswtrees's previous raid night) held throughout this entire raid - boots are enchanted on all 12 kills here.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: All 12 real bosses attempted were killed this raid night, including Kael'thas Sunstrider (a boss that went unkilled after 3 wipes on Danceswtrees's previous raid night) plus the Gruul's Lair pair (High King Maulgar, Gruul the Dragonkiller) from the tier recently added to this pipeline. Danceswtrees ranked 1st of the tracked healers present by item-level rank on 3 of 12 kills (58% average rank percentile) and 1st by raw healing on 4 of 12 - a real, more mixed showing than Danceswtrees's previous raid night. Percentiles ranged widely, from 25th (Hydross) to 86th (Maulgar), with Hydross, Kael'thas (30th), and The Lurker Below (39th) the raid's three lowest kills. Raw HPS trailed the Top 100 average on every single kill (40%-68% of the benchmark average), and overheal matched or exceeded the Top 100 sample's own single worst parse on 9 of 12 kills, with 2 more within 2 points of it - only Solarian ran comfortably clear of that bar. Lifebloom's composition share ran ahead of the Top 100 average on 8 of 12 kills; Rejuvenation led instead on Leotheras and Morogrim, and Regrowth led on Lurker and Solarian. Deaths were heavily concentrated on Leotheras (9, the most of the raid night by a wide margin), with Kael'thas and Vashj (6 each, including Danceswtrees's own single death on Vashj) also rough; Lurker and Alar were the only two clean kills. Swiftmend went uncast on 4 kills (Maulgar, Gruul, Void Reaver, Solarian) against a norm where it's used on 77-99% of parses depending on the boss, and Rebirth went uncast on Kael'thas despite 6 real deaths and a real 62% usage norm there - the raid night's most notable single cooldown gap.
RaidOverview.RAID_WARNING_BANNER: Overheal matched or exceeded the Top 100 sample's own single worst parse on 9 of 12 kills this raid night, with 2 more within 2 points of it. Combined with a raw HPS that trailed the Top 100 average on every single kill and a Lifebloom composition share running ahead of the benchmark on 8 of 12 kills, this is the same priority-target HoT-stacking pattern seen on Danceswtrees's previous raid night, more pronounced here - see each boss's own page for the real numbers.

GearCheckItems.Description: MainHand and OffHand vary on a single kill
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: Both slots swap to a fishing pole and lure on The Lurker Below only, consistent with a fight-specific tactical choice for that encounter, not a gearing gap.

GearCheckItems.Description: Elixir of Healing Power (Battle) and Elixir of Draenic Wisdom (Guardian) both active on all 12 kills, plus Well Fed food buff
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: A real, complete consumable setup across the whole raid night - the Battle + Guardian Elixir pairing is a fully valid alternative to a Flask under the real TBC rule.
